description_long: This is a vintage biplane with a wooden propeller and dual wings stacked one above the other, connected by a series of struts and wires. The fuselage is slender and tapers toward the tail, which features a conventional tail assembly with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The wings and tail surfaces are predominantly white with red checkerboard insignia and black numbering, suggesting a historical or military design, possibly Polish. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tail skid, all fixed and non-retractable. The cockpit is open and situated just behind the upper wing, with a small windshield and visible pilot seat. The overall construction appears to be a mix of wood and fabric, typical of early 20th-century aircraft.
description: A vintage biplane with dual white and red checkerboard wings, wooden propeller, and open cockpit, featuring fixed landing gear and a slender fuselage.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the dual stacked wings with red checkerboard patterns and the full length of the fuselage.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the open cockpit, wooden propeller, fixed main wheels, and the tail's checkerboard insignia.
description_view_2: Front view displaying the wooden propeller, open cockpit, and the symmetrical arrangement of the biplane's wings and struts.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the landing gear, cockpit, and the tail section with its red and white markings.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two sets of straight, high-mounted wings connected by vertical struts and wires; no winglets or noticeable dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Open, single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing, forward on the fuselage.
